content.full_text:
October 26, 1992

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Staff Members:

I have a potential problem regarding a possible conflict of interest for which I need your advice and opinion.

I am a Consulting Civil Engineer and Land Surveyor by profession (30 years) and still conduct a small limited practice. Due to my experience and expertise in the field of land use planning, design, division and development I have served several major clients during those years.

In 1984 I was retained by a landowner to assist during a General Plan revision and zoning process.

In 1991 they sold about one-half of the property with a condition that any land development plans obtain their prior approval, and I was retained to assist them in the review and approval process.

In February 1992 I was appointed by the Governor to fill an unexpired term of Plumas County Supervisor. As County Supervisor, I may be in a position to approve the land development plans of the property purchaser.

This leads to the question: Is it a conflict of interest for me to continue to serve the seller of the property when the purchaser of the property will need County approval of development plans? I receive no compensation from the purchaser of the property.

May I please hear from you; and please contact me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Paul E. Simpson
Paul E. Simpson, Supervisor
Third District
